title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in New Rochelle, NY | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in New Rochelle, Westchester. Humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Dealing with bats in your New Rochelle home or business? Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Westchester County. Our licensed technicians use humane, effective methods to safely remove bats and prevent their return to your property.
whyUs: With over a decade of experience serving New Rochelle and the greater Westchester area, our certified bat removal specialists understand local bat species and building structures. We provide thorough inspections, safe removal, complete exclusion work, and cleanup services with a satisfaction guarantee.
localContext: New Rochelle's historic homes in the North End, Tudor-style residences, and waterfront properties along Long Island Sound often attract bats seeking shelter in attics, chimneys, and eaves. The city's mature tree canopy and proximity to parks like Glen Island and Five Islands Park create ideal bat habitats, making professional exclusion services essential for property owners.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in New Rochelle?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in New Rochelle typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, entry points, and the extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal in New Rochelle regulated?
faqs.answer: Yes, bat removal in New York is regulated to protect bat populations. Removal cannot be performed during maternity season (May 1 - August 15) when flightless young are present. Our team follows all New York State DEC regulations and uses only humane exclusion methods.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my New Rochelle home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, brown staining near entry points, accumulation of guano (droppings) in attics or near the roofline, and sightings of bats entering or exiting your home at twilight. Our experts can conduct a thorough inspection to confirm bat activity.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our process includes a complete property inspection to identify entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not re-enter, sealing all potential entry points, attic cleanup and sanitization if needed, and a warranty to ensure bats don't return. The process typically takes 1-3 days with a follow-up inspection.
